behavior_summary: This script implements a beggar NPC's in-world behaviour: a short U8P_PESANT3::look/description, an attention/fast-area routine to call out to nearby players, a player 'U8P_PESANT3::use' (talk) flow that begs for donations and can accept items/coins (with a slider to choose amount) and reward larger donations with random advice barks, a U8P_PESANT3::cast/attack handler that notifies guards and shouts for help, and cleanup when an actor leaves the trigger area.
U8P_PESANT3::look returns a short description — 'dead beggar' if the NPC is dead, otherwise 'beggar'.

U8P_PESANT3::enterFastArea watches for actors entering range and (when a numeric/distance guard and not-dead checks pass) calls U8P_PESANT3::func0B53 to attract attention (short barks and spawn/timer pacing).

U8P_PESANT3::use runs the interactive begging/conversation: plays a randomized greeting, opens conversation UI (beginConversationNoMap), asks the player if they will give money, queries available quantity (Item::getQuantity), requests a donation amount via Item::getSliderInput, moves/creates donated items (FREE::findNearbyItems), thanks the player, and for sufficiently large donations selects one of several random advice barks (FREE::randomInRange0).

U8P_PESANT3::cast responds to aggression: runs a short countdown/timer loop if no target, or when there is an aggressor it calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime, spawns a timer process, clears in-combat state, and barks an alarm (two variants chosen by random).
U8P_PESANT3::leaveFastArea clears several stored references/process links (FREE::resetRef) to stop area-linked behaviors and timers.
notable_control_flow: Look path: immediate IsDead(this) check -> choose between two short bark/description outputs -> suspend.
Fast-area entry: numeric/distance check + IsDead(this) guard -> on success call U8P_PESANT3::func0B53 for a staged attention routine (randomized barks with spawn/suspend timing).

Use/talk flow: greeting -> beginConversationNoMap -> ask Yes/No (FREE::askYesNo) -> on Yes accumulate Item::getQuantity into a donation upper bound -> ask slider Item::getSliderInput for donation amount -> call FREE::findNearbyItems to effect transfer -> if donation > threshold play thanks + advice chosen by FREE::randomInRange0.

Cast flow: branch on Npc::getTarget(this) -> no-target loop uses spawn FREE::func1C19 and a countdown local -> aggressor path calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime, spawns timer, calls Npc::clrInCombat(this), then emits a randomly-chosen alarm bark.
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_PESANT3::func0B53; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: What precisely does the numeric check 'this > 0x00FAh' represent in U8P_PESANT3::enterFastArea/U8P_PESANT3::use (distance, actor ID, or another gating value)?
What exact UI mechanics FREE::findNearbyItems uses for transferring donated coins/items (stack creation vs direct inventory transfer)?
Is somebodyTalking a global flag shared across scripts (it is read to avoid interrupting speech) and what exact name/namespace does it U8P_PESANT3::use at runtime?

main_function.name: U8P_PESANT3::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when they inspect the NPC: returns 'dead beggar' if the NPC is dead, otherwise 'beggar'.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or inspects this NPC (standard U8P_PESANT3::look/inspect handler).
Checks Npc::isDead(this) to decide which short description to present.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::enterFastArea
function_map.role: triggered when actor enters NPC fast area; gate checks then call attention utility

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: cleanup: reset references/process links when actor leaves trigger area

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::cast
function_map.role: handle being targeted/attacked: announce alarm and notify guards, U8P_PESANT3::use timers for repetition

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::use
function_map.role: player interaction/talk/begging flow: greetings, ask-for-money, accept donations, give thanks/advice

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT3::func0B53
function_map.role: attention-getting utility: distance guard, randomized barks, timed spawn/suspend sequence, calls METHOD::func15E7

internal_calls.purpose: When entry checks pass, delegate to the attention routine to call out to nearby players.

external_calls.purpose: Notify the guard/AI system that a crime or attack occurred so guards can respond.
Open the conversation UI/session with the player without changing maps, used at the start of the talk flow.
Present a Yes/No prompt asking the player if they will give money; result examined with process_result().
Query how many of the offered item/coin the player can give to determine the slider upper bound.
Ask the player to choose a donation amount via a slider between 0/1 and the available quantity; result stored in a local (local3).
Transfer or create the donated items/coins near the NPC/player to effect the donation.
Select a random index to choose which advice/thank-you bark to speak after a larger donation.
Advance or finalize attention/interaction state after barks; likely notifies other subsystems or progresses conversation state.
Measure distance to the actor/player to decide whether to emit attention barks.
Spawn a short-lived timer/helper process used to pace repeated behaviour (spawn/suspend loops).
Clear stored process references (IDs) associated with the NPC so area-linked behaviours and timers stop.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Used as a guard to avoid emitting attention barks when other speech is active (prevent interrupting).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a beggar NPC's in-world behaviour: a short U8P_PESANT3::look/description, an attention/fast-area routine to call out to nearby players, a player 'U8P_PESANT3::use' (talk) flow that begs for donations and can accept items/coins (with a slider to choose amount) and reward larger donations with random advice barks, a U8P_PESANT3::cast/attack handler that notifies guards and shouts for help, and cleanup when an actor leaves the trigger area.
